name: tui-snapshot-parity
description: TUI/snapshot parity pattern: TimingPanel shows same fields as snapshot TIMING section. Fields to keep in sync: prompt (snapshot->TUI), last_thought (TUI->snapshot), step detail section (TUI->snapshot via STEP DETAIL block). Each new state field in WorkflowState must be added to both renderers.
snapshot.py step_history loss: update_state_file double-escapes JSON quotes; json.loads fails on readback, history resets to [] each step
resolution: Add _parse_step_history() in snapshot.py with unescape fallback: try raw.replace('\"', '"') if initial parse fails

content: last_thought display: suppress bare tool names (no spaces, no colon) — prism_activity_hook writes tool_name alone when no context; meaningful thoughts always have spaces or 'tool: context' format

step_history YAML frontmatter: parse_state_file uses split(':',1) so a one-line JSON array value (e.g. [{"i":0,"s":2,"tc":10}]) parses correctly — inner JSON colons are preserved in the value after the first split

name: plugin-version-display
description: Read version from .claude-plugin/plugin.json relative to tool: Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / '.claude-plugin' / 'plugin.json'. Store as module-level _PLUGIN_VERSION string. Display as 'v{version}' in TUI format_title and snapshot header. Fallback to empty string on any Exception — no crash.

name: cli-version-header
description: TUI and snapshot headers show PRISM version from .claude-plugin/plugin.json via _read_plugin_version() helper. Module-level _PLUGIN_VERSION constant with graceful fallback to empty string.

name: claude-plugin-root-version-resolution
description: CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT preferred pattern: _read_plugin_version() checks os.environ.get('CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT') first; if set, resolves plugin.json as Path(root)/'.claude-plugin'/'plugin.json'; falls back to Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent pattern when unset. Applied in both app.py and snapshot.py.

name: plugin-root-version-resolution
description: _read_plugin_version() prefers CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var for plugin.json resolution, falls back to __file__-relative. Shows the version of the actually-loaded plugin (cache or source), critical for detecting stale cache.

content: Version bump pattern: update plugin.json version field AND update test_release_docs.py AC-4 assertion (method name, docstring, and assert value) to match. Other AC sections (1-3, 5-6) test CHANGELOG content and do not need updating for patch bumps.
Every code change to prism-devtools requires at least a 0.0.1 version bump in plugin.json and corresponding test assertion update in test_release_docs.py.

name: workflow-table-bar-columns
description: Workflow table uses proportional ASCII bars (DurBar, TokBar) instead of percentage columns. _fmt_bar(value, total, width=6) returns '#'-filled, '-'-empty string. Total is computed from step_history + current live values. Phase and Type columns removed for cleaner layout.

content: DurBar/TokBar columns use Unicode block chars: '█' for filled and '░' for empty (replaces '#'/'-' ASCII). Style matches the story panel green progress bar.

snapshot activity-feed: _render_activity_feed() parses session transcript JSONL for tool_use content items; extracts timestamp, tool name, args (first 2 keys, 30-char truncation). Replaces TIMING section in snapshot output to show live tool call log.

name: activity-feed-widget
description: ActivityFeed widget: reads transcript JSONL incrementally (same pattern as _read_live_tokens), extracts tool_use from top-level type=tool_use and assistant message.content[] array, keeps last 20 entries in deque, renders via update_state(WorkflowState) interface matching all other dashboard widgets.

content: DurBar/TokBar use agent-specific Rich colors: SM=blue, QA=red, DEV=green, gate=dim. _agent_bar_color() maps agent→color. Width increased from 6 to 10 for better proportional resolution.

name: bar-scaling-gate-exclusion
description: When computing total_dur/total_toks for DurBar/TokBar proportional bars, exclude current step if gate (step_type=='gate') or stale. Gate steps accumulate hours while paused, dominating the total and making completed steps round to 0 blocks. Show empty bars for the gate row itself since gate duration is not meaningful work time.

name: storypanel-full-width-layout
description: TUI layout change: StoryPanel moved from left-col Vertical to full-width row directly in body Container, between GatePanel and details Horizontal. CSS entry added with height:auto margin-bottom:1 at top level.

content: snapshot-section-order: render_snapshot() order matches TUI layout: WORKFLOW > Gate Alert > STORY > ACTIVITY FEED > STEP DETAIL

name: storypanel-left-col-layout
description: StoryPanel moved from full-width row into #left-col Vertical after StepDetail. ActivityFeed in #right-col gets '#right-col ActivityFeed { height: 1fr }' scoped CSS rule to fill full column height.

content: GatePanel moved from full-width body row into #left-col Vertical (stacked above StepDetail and StoryPanel). Right-col remains ActivityFeed full-height. compose() order: AgentRoster, WorkflowTable, Horizontal#details(left-col[GatePanel,StepDetail,StoryPanel], right-col[ActivityFeed]).

name: gate-panel-full-width-layout
description: GatePanel layout: full-width before Horizontal(#details). GatePanel belongs outside left-col Vertical — yield GatePanel() directly under Container(#body) before the Horizontal(#details) block. In styles.tcss GatePanel gets margin-bottom:1 for spacing.

content: Phase column in WorkflowTable/snapshot: _fmt_phase() maps Planning=blue, TDD RED=red, TDD GREEN=green; completed steps use dim=True; snapshot.py uses plain f-string padding step.phase:<12; TUI/snapshot parity requires both files updated together
GatePanel is inside #left-col Vertical (first child, before StepDetail and StoryPanel). Right-col retains ActivityFeed full-height. #left-col has overflow-y:auto. Introduced in 2.6.3.

description: brain_engine._normalize() story_completeness: PSP_WEIGHTS for ('sm','draft_story') uses story_completeness=0.3, but _normalize() only divided by 100 for coverage_pct/traceability_pct/probe_accuracy — story_completeness fell through to raw value (e.g. 85.0 instead of 0.85), corrupting SM PSP scores.
resolution: Add 'story_completeness' to the percentage-metrics tuple in _normalize(). Pattern: whenever a new pct metric is added to PSP_WEIGHTS, also add it to _normalize()'s division-by-100 branch.
